title: Pathlib, Content Discovery, Roots, Symlinks, and Admission · Contract Workshop
summary: A civic-publishing pair incrementally builds bounded content-discovery manifest from original fixed content fixtures while retaining earlier Python, Git, HTML, CSS, URL, accessibility, security, determinism, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of bounded content-discovery manifest while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use Path and PurePath operations with explicit platform, resolution, relative, and I/O semantics instead of string slicing.
Declare roots, extensions, ignores, hidden files, drafts, ordering, maximum files, depth, and total bytes before traversal.
Admit or reject symlinks using resolved ancestry, cycle handling, file type, and ownership policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pathlib Semantics
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop publishing team handles case 1059 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to build a bounded content manifest with explicit rejection, root, and collision evidence; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Discovery Manifest
steps.instruction:

Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-discovery-manifest-1v12bn
# Competency: ssg-discovery-manifest.
# Case 1641: a compromised-content recovery rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def evidence_ssg_discovery_manifest_1v12bn(discovered: int = 12, admitted: int = 10, rejected: int = 2, root_escapes: int = 0, route_collisions: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1v12bn = "1v12bn-nb21v1-1641"
    assert evidence_variant_1v12bn
    evidence_axes_1v12bn = (
        "reader1v12bn",
        "source1641",
        "parsenb21v1",
        "route1v12bn1641",
        "artifact16411v12bn",
        "recoverynb21v11641",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1v12bn)) == 6
    if min(discovered, admitted, rejected) < 0:
        return False, discovered, "discovery-count"
    if admitted + rejected != discovered:
        return False, admitted, "discovery-reconciliation"
    if root_escapes > 0:
        return False, root_escapes, "root-escape"
    if route_collisions > 0:
        return False, route_collisions, "route-collision"
    assert admitted + rejected == discovered
    return True, admitted, "admitted-content-manifest"
